units.title: Unit 1: Section 1: A Look into the Past ..................................................................    1
mustKnow: acquire knowledge about the beginnings of psychology
clarify the differences between the main psychology disciplines
understand the hierarchy of needs
niceToKnow: Assignment 1 questions on pages 4 and 5 are problematic for many students, so look
Section 1:  A Look into the Past
supportingKnowledge: Assignment 1 questions on pages 4 and 5 are problematic for many students, so look
likelyMisconceptions: Students may use Look as a label without understanding how it connects to the assessed task.
Students may treat related theories or terms as interchangeable instead of naming the actual difference.

units.title: Unit 2: Section 2: The Principles of Learning  .......................................................    4
mustKnow: understand and explain the basic concepts of the learning process
likelyMisconceptions: Students may use Learning as a label without understanding how it connects to the assessed task.

units.title: Unit 3: Section 2: Thinking and Memory ..............................................................    7
mustKnow: be aware of how the brain processes information
be aware of problems with brain functions
understand the significance of memory to learning
understanding the foundations of thinking and reasoning
likelyMisconceptions: Students may use Memory as a label without understanding how it connects to the assessed task.

units.title: Unit 4: Section 3: Roles and Group Influences  ..................................................    10
mustKnow: be aware of the impact groups have on behaviour
understand the many aspects of roles
understanding how people can be viewed according to focus and approach
likelyMisconceptions: Students may use Group as a label without understanding how it connects to the assessed task.
Students may memorize terms without recognizing examples, counterexamples, or application contexts.

units.title: Unit 5: Section 3: Emotions and Criminal Behaviour  .......................................    14
mustKnow: differentiate the types of defence mechanisms
have knowledge of the criminal and unhealthy elements of emotions
understand the nature of emotions and how they effect our lives
likelyMisconceptions: Students may use Behaviour as a label without understanding how it connects to the assessed task.

units.title: Unit 6: Section 3: Neurosis ................................................................................    18
mustKnow: be aware that anxiety is the basis of all neurosis
categorize the impact of neurosis in terms of physical, mental, and emotional
understand abnormal behaviours from the context of knowing normal behaviour
likelyMisconceptions: Students may use Neurosis as a label without understanding how it connects to the assessed task.
Students may memorize terms without recognizing examples, counterexamples, or application contexts.
Students may produce chapter-note summaries instead of evidence-based explanations tied to the prompt.

units.title: Unit 7: Section 3: Psychosis  ..............................................................................    22
mustKnow: distinguish the main kinds of psychosis
identify some of the important treatments for psychotic behaviour
understand the major characteristics of psychosis
likelyMisconceptions: Students may memorize terms without recognizing examples, counterexamples, or application contexts.
Students may use Psychosis as a label without understanding how it connects to the assessed task.

units.title: Unit 8: Section 4: Mastering Your Life  .............................................................    26
mustKnow: gain an awareness of the choices available in life in a variety of dimensions
have knowledge of the careers in psychology and be aware of the personal
understand some of the consequences of our choices
likelyMisconceptions: Students may use Life as a label without understanding how it connects to the assessed task.
